summary.interpretation: Static weights show POSITIVE depth gradient (increasing algebraic capacity). Live attention shows NEGATIVE (complete convergence in deep layers). The gap = navigation space.
Static and live both negative, but live is WEAKER (-0.658 vs -0.930). Deep layers retain nonzero CV — less convergence than Pythia.

_meta.method: output_attentions=True, commutator algebra on attention matrices per layer
key_findings: ALL 10 measurements show negative live depth gradient (p < 0.002 in all cases)
Pythia-410m: SIGN REVERSAL — static r=+0.67, live r=-0.91
GPT-2-medium: same direction but weaker — static r=-0.93, live r=-0.66
Pythia deep layers: CV=0 (complete head convergence/sedimentation in action)
GPT-2 deep layers: CV>0 (still differentiated, less sedimentation)
Cross-prompt consistency: Pythia sigma=0.006, GPT-2 sigma=0.033
Architecture effect persists in live: Pythia more negative than GPT-2
Input content modulates magnitude but not direction
